description: CFG-MP Manifold Projection - Project Euler step results back toward data manifold
hypothesis: High CFG guidance causes the ODE trajectory to drift off the learned data manifold. By projecting each Euler step result back toward the on-manifold interpolation (computed from pred_x_0 and estimated noise), we can reduce off-manifold artifacts while preserving guidance strength. This addresses a fundamentally different failure mode than CFG-Zero* (scale) or APG (direction).

implementation.technique: Noise-space re-interpolation from pred_x_0 at t_prev, then linear blend toward manifold

parameter_sweep_plan.notes: Start with 0.15, sweep to find optimal. Higher values = stronger projection but may over-smooth.

scores.notes: Pending evaluation. Recommend sweeping cfg_mp_strength in [0.05, 0.3]

notes: Paper: arXiv 2601.21892 (Jan 2025). Validated on DiT-XL-2-256, Flux, SD3.5. Different mechanism from already-tested CFG-Zero*/APG. Default off (0.0) for backward compatibility. Passed via tex_slat_sampler_params kwargs.
